skill.name: ai-product-photography
skill.description: Generate product photography prompts and run inference.sh image models for studio, lifestyle, e-commerce, and marketing product images.
skill.summary: Create AI product photography workflows for e-commerce and commercial image generation.
skill.author: inference-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command-related patterns, but most Ruby backtick and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown formatting or prompt text. A real high-risk issue remains: the Quick Start pipes a remote installer directly to sh, and the skill intentionally sends prompts or image URLs to external inference services.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
high_findings.description: The Quick Start fetches an installer from cli.inference.sh and pipes it directly to sh before login. This is a real supply-chain risk because a compromised endpoint or response could execute shell code in the user environment.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present on line 21 and executes a remote installer. The domain appears related to the intended service, so this is dangerous but not evidence of confirmed malware.

medium_findings.title: Remote Image Generation CLI Usage
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs use of infsh app run to send prompts and image URLs to hosted model providers. This is intended functionality, but users may transmit private product concepts, brand details, or source image URLs to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples clearly invoke remote inference applications through the CLI. This is consistent with the skill purpose, so the risk is data exposure rather than hidden malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections correspond to Markdown code fences and inline code spans around examples, model IDs, and prompt formulas. I did not find evidence that these backticks are Ruby command substitution or hidden executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and these locations are visibly fenced or inline Markdown examples. The conclusion is high confidence for the backtick alerts, while the shell commands inside some fences still need separate review.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are metadata or product photography prompt text, not cryptographic code. I did not find hashing, encryption, or credential handling logic in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are the YAML description and natural-language image prompts. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or secret-processing context at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The Quick Start fetches an installer from cli.inference.sh and pipes it directly to sh before login. This is a real supply-chain risk because a compromised endpoint or response could execute shell code in the user environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present on line 21 and executes a remote installer. The domain appears related to the intended service, so this is dangerous but not evidence of confirmed malware.

content.value_statement: Product teams need polished images before every product is photographed in a studio. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured prompts and inference.sh commands for commercial product visuals.
actual_capabilities: Provides prompt patterns for studio, lifestyle, hero, flat lay, and action product images.
Shows inference.sh CLI commands for FLUX, Imagen 3, Grok, and Seedream image models.
Includes templates for Amazon main images, Amazon lifestyle images, and Shopify hero banners.
Outlines batch generation for multiple product angles using shell variables and infsh commands.
Describes a post-processing flow for upscaling and background removal through inference.sh apps.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI, an account, and access to the listed model providers.
Generated images may need manual review for brand accuracy, product fidelity, and marketplace compliance.
The Quick Start installer uses a pipe-to-shell pattern that should be replaced before publication.
It does not verify legal rights for logos, trademarks, product designs, or reference images.

use_cases.title: Build Listing Images
use_cases.description: Create white-background and lifestyle image prompts for marketplace listings that need consistent framing and lighting.

use_cases.title: Explore Campaign Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate hero shot and lifestyle concepts before committing to a commercial photo shoot or design brief.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Compare product angles, lighting styles, and backgrounds for new packaging or product launch materials.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Studio Product Shot
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a clean e-commerce image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prompt and inference.sh command for a studio product photo of [product]. Use a pure white background, soft lighting, and sharp focus.

prompt_templates.title: Lifestyle Product Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for context and brand mood.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a lifestyle product photography prompt for [product] in [setting]. Include lighting, props, camera angle, and a commercial style direction.

prompt_templates.title: Marketplace Image Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for several listing assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan four product images for [marketplace] using [product]. Include a main image, lifestyle image, detail shot, and scale image with inference.sh commands.

prompt_templates.title: Full Post-Processing Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that includes generation and refinement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete AI product photography workflow for [product]. Include model choice, prompt variants, batch angles, upscaling, and background removal steps.

output_examples.input: Wireless earbuds for an online store main image.
output_examples.output: A clean studio prompt with a white surface, soft shadows, high detail, and a matching inference.sh model command.

output_examples.input: Skincare bottle for a premium brand campaign.
output: Hero shot direction with premium lighting

output_examples.input: Coffee mug for a Shopify homepage banner.
output_examples.output: A wide hero image concept with morning light, desk context, negative space for copy, and commercial styling notes.

best_practices: Review all generated images for product fidelity before using them in customer-facing materials.
Use consistent lighting, camera angle, and background language across a full product image set.
Replace pipe-to-shell installation instructions with a safer verified installation process.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential unreleased product details to remote image services without approval.
Do not use generated marketplace images without checking platform rules and product accuracy.
Do not rely on brand-name style references when trademark or endorsement confusion is possible.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images directly?
faq.answer: It provides prompts and inference.sh CLI commands that call external image generation models.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as written?
faq.answer: No. The remote installer piped to sh should be removed or replaced before publication.

faq.question: Can it create Amazon listing concepts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes main image and lifestyle image prompt patterns for Amazon-style listings.

faq.question: Can it process existing product images?
faq.answer: It includes post-processing examples for upscaling and background removal when image URLs are available.

faq.question: What should users check before using outputs?
faq.answer: Users should verify product accuracy, brand rights, marketplace rules, and privacy requirements.
